summary: Set 036 Session 2 of 7 — new_chat_id CLI + Claude Code hook-invoker pass-through (Q1 native-source wiring). Shipped: (1) ai_router/new_chat_id.py — agent-facing UUID v4 CLI for orchestrators with no native per-chat session-id surface; --export emits shell-eval-able line via bash / powershell / fish formatters with single-quote escape hardening; --shell auto-detect consults $SHELL first on every platform (Round A Major fix); idempotent against existing $CHAT_SESSION_ID; EXIT_SHELL_DETECT_FAILED=1 when --export hits an undetectable shell with no --shell. (2) claude-session-start-invoker.js — extracts session_id from SessionStart payload via new extractSessionId() (trim + non-empty gate; null on missing/malformed); forwards as --chat-session-id to start_session; preserveExistingClaude() now gates model/effort preservation on the full H4 triple including chatSessionId (Round B Medium fix: was only engine+provider, which incorrectly preserved a different Claude chat's gauges); tolerant branches for legacy key-absent + Set-036-null cases mirror start_session.py's H3 predicate; stderr warning on missing session_id (Round B Low fix: R2 schema-drift visibility). (3) installOrchestratorHookGemini.ts + installOrchestratorHookCopilot.ts — invoke shared maybeShowNewChatIdWorkflowToast helper before checkOutOrchestrator quickpick; one-time-per-(workspace, orchestrator) info toast with three clipboard-copy actions: bash (current-shell `eval "$(cmd)"` form per Round B Major fix — pipeline-subshell variant doesn't persist export), PowerShell (Invoke-Expression), fish (Round B Medium fix: was omitted despite being in R3 initial scope). (4) src/commands/newChatIdWorkflowToast.ts (new) — shared toast helper with workspaceState suppression. Tests: 26 unit tests in ai_router/tests/test_new_chat_id.py (22 initial + 4 added during Round A); 22 Layer-2 tests in src/test/suite/claudeSessionStartInvoker.test.ts (13 initial + 9 added during Round B for preserveExistingClaude). Full Python suite: 686 passed + 1 skipped (was 682 pre-Session-2). Full extension Layer-2: 484 passed; 2 pre-existing scaffolding failures unrelated to Session 2 (configEditor-foundation panel lifecycle + notificationsSection rendering). tsc --noEmit clean. Verification: Round A via gpt-5-4 ($0.197865, 149s, Python CLI bundle) — 1 Major (Windows shell-detect over-eager) + 1 Minor (fish escape untested), both fixed. Round B via gpt-5-4 ($0.2651975, 195s, JS+TS bundle) — 1 Major (broken pipeline-subshell eval form) + 2 Medium (fish copy command missing + preserveExistingClaude missing H4 composite) + 1 Low (no R2 stderr signal), all four fixed. Cumulative Session 2 routed spend: $0.463 ($0.198 + $0.265). Cumulative Set 036 spend (Sessions 1+2): $1.091 of $5 NTE. Bundled fix: CI workflow `actions/upload-artifact@v3 → v4` (commit eca6a96) — pre-existing deprecation breaking the Playwright matrix on every push; addressed mid-session as a standalone hotfix. Bundled fix: Set 011 (cancelled) state-file conformance to Set 035 H2 (commit 637c5c0) — flipped status to 'cancelled' to match CANCELLED.md presence, scrubbed two duplicate timestamp entries from CANCELLED.md that a buggy re-cancel double-append surfaced. Memory note added on the cancel-lifecycle double-append behavior for a future session set. Out of scope for Session 2 (deferred to Sessions 3–7): signalKind retirement + Codex config-toml watcher retirement (S3); Takeover UX modal/CLI prompt + watcher-inventory test (S4); Layer-3 Playwright coverage + cross-tier docs + cross-repo notice (S5); orchestrator-agnostic UI audit + empty-state refactor (S6); final tests + change-log + dual-registry release (S7).
files_changed: docs/session-sets/011-readme-polish/CANCELLED.md (bundled hotfix — commit 637c5c0, scrub of duplicate timestamps)

reason.specifics: Session 3 retires the signalKind enum from the OrchestratorAccordion UI surface AND deletes the Codex config-toml watcher entirely per the D1 watcher-scope discipline locked in the audit-addendum. The work spans both the extension TS (accordion render simplification, marker view-model collapse, CSS sweep) and the watcher-source deletion (src/codex/configWatcher.ts removed, extension.ts safeRegister call removed). Same orchestrator preserves the Set-033/Set-036 design context — the Q5 lifecycle lock from Session 1, the chatSessionId Q1 plumbing from Session 2, and the upcoming Q3 takeover UX (Session 4) all sit downstream of Session 3's watcher-scope cleanup, so a fresh orchestrator would need extensive re-orientation. The empty-state CTA refactor (Session 6) and Layer-3 coverage (Session 5) further benefit from a single Claude session carrying the full architectural through-line.
